title: TypeScript is not TypeScript anymore...
summary: TypeScript 7 rewrites the compiler from TypeScript into Go, achieving approximately 10 times faster type checking. The release candidate introduces shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode while maintaining compatibility with existing error outputs and file formats.
writeup: The video covers TypeScript 7, which rewrites the compiler from TypeScript into Go and reaches roughly 10 times faster type checking. This matters because the rewrite changes the compiler's foundation while preserving compatibility with existing error outputs and file formats. Concrete takeaways: the release candidate adds shared memory parallelism, new checkers and builders flags, and a rewritten watch mode.
